content.definition: Simplicity is the virtue of living without pretense or excess, ordering your heart and home toward what truly matters. It means stripping away unnecessary complications so that Christ can be more clearly seen and served in your daily life.
content.thomistic_note: Aquinas teaches that simplicity is fundamentally an act of the will aligned with truth, opposing the duplicity and artificiality that fragment the soul. In the context of Holy Week, simplicity becomes a participation in Christ's kenosis—the self-emptying that precedes redemption. The simple heart, unburdened by the need to impress or accumulate, becomes the fitting vessel for grace.
practices: During Holy Week, reduce your home's visual and auditory distractions—dim unnecessary lights, silence notifications, use simple flowers or bare branches rather than elaborate decorations—to create space for deeper prayer and presence with your family.
Prepare one simple meal each day this week using basic ingredients, involving your children in the preparation as a shared act of simplicity and service rather than culinary display.
Examine one area of your homeschool materials or home and remove items that complicate rather than serve your family's formation; donate or discard with gratitude for what was useful.

Choose one simple spiritual practice for Holy Week—such as a single decade of the rosary, one psalm, or five minutes of silence—rather than attempting multiple devotions, modeling the power of singular focus.
content.saint_story: Thérèse, a young Carmelite nun, discovered what she called her 'little way'—a path of spiritual childhood and radical simplicity. She did not perform great penances or spectacular virtues, but rather found holiness in small, hidden acts done with love: folding linens carefully, listening patiently to a sister's complaints, offering God the tiny sacrifices of daily convent life. When others asked about her spiritual methods, she spoke of simplicity itself as her method. By Holy Week each year, she would renew her commitment to this way, seeing in Christ's passion not a call to grand gestures but to faithful presence in small things. Her wisdom has comforted countless mothers: that a life of simple, hidden love is not less than a life of apparent accomplishment, but more, because it is rooted in truth.
content.daily_prompt: What is one layer of complication I can remove today—in my schedule, my speech, my expectations—to make more room for Christ's presence in my family?
content.examen_question: When did I feel most at peace today, and what was simple about that moment? When did I feel scattered or burdened, and what unnecessary thing was I carrying?
content.morning_encouragement: In this holy week, remember that your faithful, simple presence with your children and your God is already enough, already precious, already redemptive.
